goal_as_stated: Distill Fable-tier judgment into the local coding harness before the window closes: freeze gold eval sets for the four judgment-heavy steps (decomposition quality, replan, review findings, test-gap severity), build an eval harness (agents/evals/) with Fable-independent graders and a scorecard runner against the real router, and tighten prompts + few-shot exemplars so the local coder tier measurably improves against a captured baseline.
restated_goal: Same goal, sequenced as a vertical slice first: land the harness core plus the REPLAN gold set end-to-end (fixtures → grader → scorecard → captured coder baseline) before fanning out to the other three steps. The replan step has a ready-made real fixture (the 3a6360cb validation failure) and the most deterministic grading, so it proves the whole loop cheaply; the other steps then reuse a working harness instead of landing into an unproven one. Gold-set and exemplar authoring stays Fable-in-session (Manual); only plumbing is worker-eligible.
inventory_summary: No eval harness exists anywhere in the repo (grep for eval hits only incidental 'evaluate' strings). The four judgment prompts are exactly where the brief says: FRAMING/DECOMPOSE/BOUNDEDNESS/REPLAN_SYSTEM in agents/coding_pipeline/architect.py; FINDINGS/CONFIRM/CONSOLIDATE_SYSTEM in agents/coding_pipeline/verify.py plus the pr_review_ensemble prompts; finder/skeptic/dedup prompts in agents/testing_ensemble/prompts.py. All four run through agents/shared/panel.py::structured (or run_member_panel), and StructuredResult.raw (added in 3a6360cb) already exposes raw model output for grading. Fixture sources are real and on disk: pipeline-runs/build/ (2 waves, journal, a real confirmed-finding replan) and pipeline-runs/smoke-tempconv/ (9 waves, framing, tree, inventory). Forge: the Coding Pipeline feature is 34/34 Done — this epic is additive, no task-level overlap. The LLM Router project's 'Model Eval' feature (6 Ready) is candidate-model bring-up (OCR/ASR/VLM on Sparks), not judgment evals — no duplication, but the scorecard runner built here (takes a model id, reports pass rates) is exactly the tool those tasks will want later: a free synergy, not a scope change.
gap_analysis: What's missing is everything above the shared plumbing: (1) a fixture format + loader for gold cases; (2) per-step graders that run WITHOUT Fable — deterministic checks, set-overlap P/R, rank correlation, and frozen-rubric checklists; (3) a scorecard runner that drives a chosen model through the REAL structured()/panel path against every gold set and reports per-step pass rates; (4) the gold sets themselves — Fable-authored reference outputs, the non-delegable half; (5) tightened prompts + few-shot exemplars, landed only after a baseline scorecard exists so uplift is measured. Grading-method calls per step: REPLAN = schema validity through the real path + expected-action-set match (kinds, targeted leaves, no touching escalated leaves) + LeafSpec structural floors; DECOMPOSITION = deterministic structure (dep graph acyclic, worker-shaped tag floors, comma-free, files named) + a frozen Fable-authored rubric checklist per case (umbrella-leaf detection is the heart); REVIEW FINDINGS = precision/recall vs a frozen reference finding set over seeded-bug diffs, matched by per-case keyword/file match rules, graded on the collect→confirm funnel's confirmed output; TEST-GAP = recall of must-find gaps + rank correlation of severity ordering vs a frozen reference, plus a cry-wolf penalty for high-severity trivia. Where a rubric is irreducibly judgment-shaped it is frozen as an explicit checklist a deterministic scorer applies — never a live-Fable judge.

options.name: A — Full four-step harness, replan slice first (recommended)
options.summary: Build agents/evals/ (fixture loader, graders, scorecard runner, meta evals CLI), author gold sets for all four steps, capture the coder baseline, then land tightened prompts + exemplars and re-score. Sequenced so harness core + replan gold set + baseline land as wave 1.
options.tradeoffs: Most leaves (~17) and the longest tail, but delivers both halves of the mission — durable regression harness AND measured uplift — across every judgment step while the Fable window is open. The vertical-slice ordering caps integration risk.

options.name: B — Two steps only (replan + review findings)
options.summary: Same harness, but gold sets only for the two most deterministically gradable steps. Decomposition and test-gap follow as a later epic.
options.tradeoffs: Faster to a measured result and lower authoring load, but decomposition is the single highest-leverage distillation target (umbrella leaves are the #1 coder failure) and deferring it risks the Fable window closing with the most valuable judgment uncaptured.

options.name: C — Rubric-only distillation (no harness)
options.summary: Skip graders/scorecards; just tighten the four prompts, add few-shot exemplars, and check frozen rubric docs into the repo.
options.tradeoffs: Cheapest, but violates the mission's core constraint: uplift would be asserted, not measured, and nothing catches future prompt/model regressions. This is exactly the 'one-time output' failure mode the brief exists to avoid.

options.name: D — Generic eval harness for all agents
options.summary: Design the harness around a plugin grader interface covering the content agents (researcher, book, video) from day one.
options.tradeoffs: Over-general: content-agent grading has different shapes (citations, prose quality) and the brief explicitly scopes them out. A coding-harness-shaped MVP whose loader/grader seams are merely *clean* leaves the door open without paying the abstraction tax now.

recommendation: Option A. The four steps are the brief's scope and all four have viable Fable-independent grading; the only real risk is sequencing, which the replan-first vertical slice addresses. Hard constraints carried into decomposition: (1) gold reference outputs and few-shot exemplars are Manual leaves — Fable authors them in-session, never the coder tier; (2) graders never call Fable — deterministic / set-overlap / rank-correlation / frozen-rubric only; (3) the baseline scorecard leaf BLOCKS every prompt-tightening leaf, so uplift is measured against a captured before; (4) agent-side changes are prompts + exemplars only, no behavior changes; (5) each gold set holds out ~1/3 of its cases from exemplar authoring so prompt tightening is scored against unseen cases, not its own few-shots.
value_ordering: Harness core: fixture schema + loader + scorecard runner + meta evals CLI (runs against the real router via panel.structured)
Replan gold set (Fable-authored, incl. the 3a6360cb raw fixture) + deterministic replan grader
Baseline scorecard of the coder tier captured and checked in for all landed gold sets
Review-findings gold set (seeded-bug diffs) + set-overlap P/R grader
Tightened prompts + few-shot exemplars for all four steps, each landed with a before/after scorecard delta
Docs + DEVELOPMENT-WORKFLOWS wiring (how to re-run the scorecard when prompts or router models change)
risks: Gold-set rot: prompts and wire schemas evolve; fixtures must pin the schema version they were authored against, and the loader should fail loudly on drift
Nondeterminism: router models at nonzero temperature make pass-rates noisy — the runner needs pinned temperature and N-repeat majority/pass@k reporting or the baseline is not a baseline
Overfitting to gold: tightening prompts against the same cases they are scored on inflates uplift — held-out cases are the guard
Prompt-budget creep: few-shot exemplars swell system prompts (decompose already runs 16k max_tokens); token cost must be part of the scorecard delta
Fable window closes mid-epic: gold authoring is the critical path and only needs the fixture schema, not the full harness — author gold immediately after the schema leaf lands
Semantic finding-matching is the weakest grader link: keyword/file match rules per case must be authored carefully or review P/R numbers lie in both directions
